description: Make task-mgr's stdout report writes panic-safe. A spawned Node `claude` child can flip the parent's stdout open file description to O_NONBLOCK (via inherited stderr aliased onto fd 1 by `2>&1`); a subsequent large bare print! then returns EAGAIN and panics. Replace the three bare-print sites in handlers.rs with a libc-backed helper that clears O_NONBLOCK before a blocking write_all, swallows BrokenPipe, and exits(1) on other errors.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: All scoped tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing public APIs — output_result/output_json keep their () signatures
Every `unsafe` block carries a `// SAFETY:` comment per the existing claude.rs convention
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
A catch-WouldBlock-and-retry loop that re-writes the full buffer (write_all's partial-write semantics duplicate the already-flushed prefix) or busy-spins
Swallowing genuine write errors (not BrokenPipe/WouldBlock) silently — they must still exit(1) with a message
Changing the () return signatures of output_result/output_json (would ripple to 59 call sites)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: clear_nonblocking clears O_NONBLOCK when set — unit test sets it via fcntl(F_SETFL) on a pipe write end, calls clear_nonblocking, asserts F_GETFL & O_NONBLOCK == 0
Positive: write_all_blocking delivers a 128 KB payload in full over a preset-O_NONBLOCK pipe write end with a draining reader thread — assert all bytes received, no panic, no error

Negative control (recreate the bug): with the write end O_NONBLOCK and the 64 KB pipe full (no reader draining), a raw `write_all` of the 128 KB payload returns ErrorKind::WouldBlock — proves the regression mechanism so the fix test can't silently pass
Positive: BrokenPipe is swallowed — close the read end before writing, call write_all_blocking on the write end, assert it returns normally (no panic, no process exit)
Negative: output_result and output_json keep `()` signatures — no changes at the 59 call sites in main.rs (grep-verify call sites still compile unchanged)
Negative: the JSON *serialization* error arm in output_json still does eprintln! + process::exit(1) (unchanged)
Positive: every new `unsafe` block has a `// SAFETY:` comment
Failure mode: if F_GETFL returns -1, clear_nonblocking does not panic and the write is still attempted
Scoped gate green: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test handlers all pass
userStories.description:
Add a libc-backed helper to src/handlers.rs that writes report text to stdout without panicking, and route the three existing bare-print sites through it.

ROOT CAUSE: `curate dedup` (and any command) prints its final report via a bare `print!` (handlers.rs:151). When invoked as `... 2>&1 | tee`, fd 1 and fd 2 share one open file description; the spawned Node `claude` child inherits stderr (runner.rs:576, Stdio::inherit) and libuv marks the shared OFD O_NONBLOCK. The large report write then returns EAGAIN and bare print! panics (exit masked to 0 by tee without pipefail). The panic is strictly downstream of curate_dedup() returning, so the DB result is already complete — only the print fails.

DO:
- Add `fn clear_nonblocking(fd: libc::c_int)`: `libc::fcntl(fd, F_GETFL)`; if `flags >= 0 && (flags & O_NONBLOCK) != 0`, `libc::fcntl(fd, F_SETFL, flags & !O_NONBLOCK)`. Wrap in `unsafe` with a `// SAFETY:` comment (mirror the libc/raw-fd style already in src/loop_engine/claude.rs::open_pty_for_child_output).
- Add `fn write_all_blocking<W: std::io::Write>(fd: libc::c_int, w: &mut W, bytes: &[u8])`: call clear_nonblocking(fd) FIRST, then `w.write_all(bytes).and_then(|_| w.flush())`; `Ok(())` => done; `Err(e) if e.kind()==BrokenPipe` => return quietly; `Err(e)` => `eprintln!("Error: failed to write to stdout: {}", e); process::exit(1)`.
- Add `fn write_stdout(s: &str)`: lock stdout, call `write_all_blocking(libc::STDOUT_FILENO, &mut lock, s.as_bytes())`.
- Rewrite the 3 sites: handlers.rs:151 `print!("{}", result.format_text())` -> `write_stdout(&result.format_text())`; handlers.rs:166 `print!("{}", format_migrate_text(result, action))` -> `write_stdout(&format_migrate_text(result, action))`; handlers.rs:200 (output_json) `Ok(json) => println!("{}", json)` -> `Ok(json) => write_stdout(&format!("{}\n", json))`.
- Keep the existing `eprintln!` + `process::exit(1)` for the JSON *serialization* error in output_json untouched (real programmer error, distinct from a transient write).

DO NOT:
- Do NOT catch WouldBlock and retry the full buffer (duplicates the already-flushed prefix) or busy-spin — clear the flag up front and do one blocking write.
- Do NOT change the `()` return types of output_result / output_json (would ripple to 59 call sites in main.rs).
- Do NOT touch the stderr path (emit_prefixed_lines / loop-engine streaming) — explicitly out of scope; it already swallows write errors.
- Do NOT add nix/rustix — libc="0.2" is already a direct dep and is the house style.

Edge cases:
- fd already blocking: clear_nonblocking is a cheap no-op (GETFL shows flag unset, skip SETFL).
- F_GETFL fails (returns -1): skip the clear, attempt the write anyway (don't error on the diagnostic).
- Reader closed (BrokenPipe): return quietly, no panic, no exit.
- Payload larger than the 64 KB pipe buffer with a slow reader: blocking write must complete in full, not EAGAIN.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Catch-WouldBlock-and-retry on the SAME locked writer: write_all already consumed an unknown prefix before the error, so retrying `bytes` duplicates output. The proactive-clear design avoids this entirely.
edgeCases: fd already blocking: clear_nonblocking is a no-op (skip SETFL)
F_GETFL returns -1: skip clear, still attempt write, do not panic
Payload > 64 KB pipe buffer with slow reader: blocking write completes in full
Empty string: write_stdout("") writes nothing, no error

failureModes.cause: Child process re-flips O_NONBLOCK between clear and write (concurrent live children)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Residual EAGAIN caught by the Err(_) => exit(1) backstop — never unwinds/panics; documented as best-effort under concurrency

failureModes.cause: Downstream reader (| head, | less) closes the pipe
failureModes.expectedBehavior: BrokenPipe swallowed, return normally, no panic

userStories.notes: All changes in src/handlers.rs. Mirror libc + std::os::fd usage from src/loop_engine/claude.rs::open_pty_for_child_output (libc::openpty + unsafe + SAFETY comments). Reuse the existing output_result match shape (handlers.rs:145-154), output_migrate_result (~:160-166), output_json (:198-206). DI seam: write_all_blocking takes (fd, &mut W: Write, bytes) so tests drive it over a libc::pipe() without touching real stdout. Test fd ownership: wrap ONLY the write end in File via from_raw_fd (File owns it, closes on drop); close the read end manually from the side the reader thread doesn't own — avoid double-close/EBADF. Linux 64 KB pipe buffer is the stable default; 128 KB payload guarantees it fills. Learning [3068]/[693]: expect()/documented-invariant IO is house style — but here prefer explicit Result handling since the whole point is not to panic. Learning [3129]: write commands emit context headers to stderr first; stdout is data only — unaffected here. Learning [3813]: output types register via impl_text_formattable!; format_text() returns String, write it as-is.
qualityDimensions: Correctness: clearing O_NONBLOCK before a single blocking write_all eliminates EAGAIN without the partial-write duplication of a retry loop
Robustness: BrokenPipe handled gracefully (standard `| head` behavior); genuine errors still surface + exit(1)
Testability: write_all_blocking is fd-parameterized so tests exercise it over a real pipe with no global-stdout coupling
Idiomatic: unsafe libc usage matches claude.rs (SAFETY comments, std::os::fd wrappers); no new crates
Surgical: changes confined to src/handlers.rs; no call-site ripple (() signatures preserved)
userStories.title: Panic-safe stdout writes via O_NONBLOCK-clearing helper

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: all three sites (handlers.rs ~:151, ~:166, ~:200) write through write_stdout; grep confirms no remaining bare print!/println! of large report text in output_result/output_migrate_result/output_json
Positive: FULL cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check all clean (no scope filter)
Positive: unsafe block has correct F_GETFL/F_SETFL bit math (clears, never sets, O_NONBLOCK) and a SAFETY comment; no other fd flags touched
Negative: no unwrap() introduced in the new code paths; output_result/output_json signatures unchanged (() return)
Negative: no dead-code / unused-import warnings for the new helpers
Documentation: consider a one-line CLAUDE.md note under a stdout/output gotcha if warranted (optional — only if it earns its place)
Pre-existing test failures fixed (or, if >~12 clearly-unrelated, spawned as a single FIX-xxx with verifyCommand and BLOCKED for human routing)
If issues found: FIX-xxx tasks spawned via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.description:
Review the handlers.rs change for quality, security (unsafe correctness), integration wiring, and documentation needs. RUN THE FULL QUALITY GATE (unscoped test suite).

DO: Verify the three bare-print sites are all routed through write_stdout; verify no call site of output_result/output_json broke (() signatures preserved); verify the unsafe fcntl block is correct (F_GETFL/F_SETFL bit math, SAFETY comment) and the BrokenPipe/error contract matches the plan; run full cargo test / clippy / fmt with NO scope filter and fix every failure including pre-existing.
DO NOT: Skip the full unscoped suite — this is THE place it runs. Do NOT scope the test run.
userStories.notes: Spawn fixes via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` — DB + JSON synced atomically, no manual edit. If no issues: emit `<task-status>REVIEW-001:done</task-status>` with 'Clean review' note. End-to-end smoke: build, then run a command through a pipe (e.g. `task-mgr curate count | cat`) and confirm full output + exit 0; the original failing shape is `task-mgr curate dedup 2>&1 | tee`.
qualityDimensions: unsafe fcntl correctness (bit math + SAFETY comment)
All three print sites wired through the helper
Full suite green including any pre-existing failures
userStories.title: Code review + final verification
